title: Humidity Threshold Exceedance (ASHRAE)
spl:
index=environment sourcetype="sensor:humidity"
| where humidity_pct > 60 OR humidity_pct < 40
| eval duration_bucket=if(humidity_pct>60,"high","low")
| stats count by zone, duration_bucket
description: Stricter alerting for short excursions supports tape media and corrosion-sensitive gear beyond static high/low limits.
value: Stricter alerting for short excursions supports tape media and corrosion-sensitive gear beyond static high/low limits.
implementation: Use sliding windows to alert only if out of 40–60% RH for >15 minutes. Pair with dew point for condensation risk.
visualization: Line chart (RH with bands), Table (zones in excursion), Single value (max excursion minutes).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: SNMP sensors.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="sensor:humidity"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Use sliding windows to alert only if out of 40–60% RH for >15 minutes. Pair with dew point for condensation risk.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=environment sourcetype="sensor:humidity"
| where humidity_pct > 60 OR humidity_pct < 40
| eval duration_bucket=if(humidity_pct>60,"high","low")
| stats count by zone, duration_bucket
```

Understanding this SPL

**Humidity Threshold Exceedance (ASHRAE)** — Stricter alerting for short excursions supports tape media and corrosion-sensitive gear beyond static high/low limits.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="sensor:humidity"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SNMP sensors. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: environment; **sourcetype**: sensor:humidity. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=environment, sourcetype="sensor:humidity". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where humidity_pct > 60 OR humidity_pct < 40` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **duration_bucket** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by zone, duration_bucket** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (RH with bands), Table (zones in excursion), Single value (max excursion minutes).
grandmaExplanation: Stricter alerting for short excursions supports tape media and corrosion-sensitive gear beyond static high/low limits — so power, cooling, and physical access problems are found before they hurt uptime.
